title: catha edulis
intro: Catha edulis, also known as khat or kat, is a flowering plant native to the Horn of Africa and the Arabian Peninsula. It contains cathinone, a stimulant that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including pain relief, fever reduction, and antimalarial effects. The plant has various health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving mood, and increasing alertness, although it can also be addictive. Catha edulis is traditionally prepared in various herbal forms, including fresh leaves, dried leaves, and tea, which are often consumed for their medicinal and stimulant effects.
